review_comment: why do we use `_async_refresh`? And not a public function?

As written in the description: to only warn but not raise an error.

Otherwise the user will both have an error and a warn.

Because `async_refresh` log_failures=True !
Oh It didn't dawn on me that that was what you meant with that

I may be rude, sorry 😅, I actually refactored the code 2-3 times to be as clean, and I was surprised when not seeing it in the patch release 😆 

Thanks 🙏
